signature: Parameter families of piecewise-affine expanding maps with "matching" (one-sided orbits of the discontinuity merging with equal derivative products), dense self-similar solvable parameter windows (piecewise-constant invariant densities, entropy plateaus, tuning/renormalization), and a fractal measure-zero bifurcation set. Hunted: alpha-continued-fraction matching (Nakada–Natsui, Carminati–Tiozzo), matching for shifted/generalised beta-transformations βx+α mod 1 (Bruin–Carminati–Kalle and successors), intermediate beta-shifts of finite type, signed binary expansion families, and fractal parameter-dependence of transport coefficients in statistical physics. Target question: does a natural 1-parameter family contain a distinguished point conjugate to the Collatz circle caricature x↦(3x+δ)/2 mod 1 with matching/solvable parameters accumulating at it from both sides?

finds.ref: Henk Bruin, Carlo Carminati, Charlene Kalle, "Matching for generalised β-transformations", Indagationes Mathematicae 28 (2017) 55–73, arXiv:1610.01872
finds.the_theorem: Family T_α(x)=βx+α (mod 1), β>1 fixed, α∈[0,1]; matching := ∃n with T_α^n(0)=T_α^n(1) (same n on both sides). (a) Section 3 remark: if matching holds then D_k := T^k(1)−T^k(0) satisfies D_0=1, D_{k+1}=βD_k−c_k with c_k∈Z, so D_m=0 forces β^m−c_1β^{m−1}−…−c_m=0: β must be an ALGEBRAIC INTEGER. (b) Theorem 1.1: for β a quadratic algebraic number, T_α exhibits matching iff β is Pisot; then β²−kβ±d=0 (d∈N, k>d±1) and the bifurcation set A_β ⊂ [0,1] of non-matching α has dim_H = log d/log β (<1); matching α form an open dense set. (c) Matching implies the acip density is piecewise constant. Multinacci β: matching on the whole fiber. Salem β: matching observed numerically, prevalence unproven.
finds.the_match: Our circle caricature x↦(3x+δ)/2 mod 1 IS the fiber β=3/2, α=δ/2 of exactly this family. Instantiating (a) at β=3/2: the numerator of D_m is 3^m−2c_1 3^{m−1}−…−2^m c_m ≡ 3^m ≡ 1 (mod 2), never zero — matching fails for EVERY α on the Collatz fiber, by the same 2⊥3 coprimality that blocks Collatz cycles. So the caricature is not a distinguished point with solvable windows accumulating along its own fiber: the entire rational-slope fiber is structurally exceptional. LOOSE PART: the caricature is not conjugate to the 2-adic Collatz map (entropy log(3/2) vs log 2, drift sign flipped); only the parity-itinerary formalism and the 2–3 arithmetic survive the caricature. The universal negative statements (no matching, any α) do transfer to the caricature; the a.e.-parameter solvability results say nothing about integer orbits (Lebesgue-null).
finds.transfer: Prove: for every α, T_{3/2,α} admits no matching, hence no piecewise-constant invariant density and no Markov partition — the invariant density of every Collatz circle caricature has infinitely many jump discontinuities (infinite prematching partition). Then quantify: classify the countable set of α with WEAK matching (T^m(0)=T^n(1), m≠n, allowed since α((3/2)^m−(3/2)^n)∈Q(3/2) is solvable) and show these give piecewise-smooth densities with non-cancelling jump ratios (3/2)^{m−n} but never parameter WINDOWS — i.e., exactly how much solvability survives at the Collatz slope: countable points, no intervals.

finds.ref: Henk Bruin, Carlo Carminati, Stefano Marmi, Alessandro Profeti, "Matching in a family of piecewise affine interval maps", Nonlinearity 32:1 (2019) 172–208, arXiv:1707.07488
finds.the_theorem: Theorem 1: ANY piecewise affine, eventually expanding circle map satisfying the matching condition (orbits of one-sided limits of each discontinuity merge with equal one-sided derivative products) has acip density constant on the elements of the prematching partition. Theorem 3: in their family Q_γ (Botella-Soler type, expanding slope s), if matching holds for some γ then s is an algebraic integer; verbatim: "In particular matching cannot hold for any non-integer rational value s" — proof via d_0=1, d_{j+1}=−s d_j+k_j, k_j∈Z, monic p_m(s)=0. Theorem 4: for integer s≥2 the bifurcation set E has zero Lebesgue measure, dim_H(E)=1, but dim_H(E∖[0,δ))<1 for all δ>0. Theorem 5: each matching interval is reconstructed from its 'pseudocenter' via shortest even s-adic expansions (explicit self-similar fractal window structure); entropy is monotone on windows by sign of matching index Δ=κ+−κ−, constant iff Δ=0 ('neutral matching'), with self-similar entropy plateaus (Theorems 7 & 10).
finds.the_match: This is the published, Collatz-blind theorem stating precisely "what fails at the Collatz-like parameter": slope 3/2 is the flagship non-integer rational, and the failure mechanism (monic integer recursion ⇒ algebraic integer slope) is the caricature-level shadow of integrality. For integer slopes (the 'solvable Collatz cousins' like pure 2x or 3x dynamics) Theorems 4–5 give the full advertised picture: dense self-similar solvable windows + measure-zero dimension-1 fractal exceptional set. LOOSE PART: their family has one non-expanding branch (slope 1) and matching between upper/lower orbits of one discontinuity; our family has two branches of slope 3/2 — but their Theorem 1 is stated for arbitrary piecewise-affine eventually expanding circle maps, so it applies verbatim to our caricature; only Theorems 4–5 (window structure) are family-specific.
finds.transfer: Two transfers: (1) use the pseudocenter/s-adic machinery at integer s to build the exact catalogue of solvable windows for the degree-2 and degree-3 'relatives' of the caricature, giving a rigorous boundary of the solvable zone whose limit point the 3/2-fiber is; (2) adapt their monicity proof to every functional we care about: any 'finite certificate' condition on the caricature that implies an identity T^m(0)=T^m(1)-type relation is void at slope 3/2 — a template lemma for ruling out whole classes of finite-state certificates.

finds.ref: Bing Li, Tuomas Sahlsten, Tony Samuel, Wolfgang Steiner, "Denseness of intermediate β-shifts of finite type", Proc. Amer. Math. Soc. 147:5 (2019) 2045–2055, arXiv:1709.08035
finds.the_theorem: For the 2-parameter family T_{β,α}(x)=βx+α mod 1 on Δ={β∈(1,2), 0≤α≤2−β}: Theorem 1.1: the set of (β,α) whose kneading space Ω_{β,α} is a subshift of finite type is DENSE in Δ (hence sofic parameters dense). Necessary conditions stated: Ω_{β,α} SFT requires β to be the maximal root of a polynomial with coefficients in {−1,0,1} (in particular an algebraic integer), and β must be an n-th root of a Perron number; for transcendental β the SFT-fiber is EMPTY. Open question posed: if β is an n-th root of a Perron number, is the SFT set dense in the fiber [0,2−β]?
finds.the_match: Direct answer to Simon's accumulation question, in the exact caricature family. Solvable (SFT/Markov, hence explicit piecewise-constant density) parameters are dense in the PLANE, so they accumulate at every point of the Collatz fiber β=3/2 from both sides — but only transversally: every solvable approximant must perturb the slope off 3/2, and the fiber itself contains no SFT parameter (3/2 is not a root of any {−1,0,1}-leading-coefficient-±1 polynomial since such roots are algebraic integers). Also note: Pisot slopes cannot accumulate at 3/2 either, since the Pisot set is closed (Salem) and 3/2∉it — so within-slope-direction accumulation of PROVEN matching-prevalent slopes is unavailable. LOOSE PART: SFT-freeness does not single out 3/2 — transcendental fibers are SFT-free too; what distinguishes the Collatz fiber is the arithmetic mechanism (mod-2 obstruction, kneading orbits living in Z[1/2] with Collatz-like denominator dynamics), not bare unsolvability.
finds.transfer: Concrete theorem to attempt: 'For β=3/2 and every α, Ω_{3/2,α} is not sofic.' Route: sofic ⇒ both kneading invariants eventually periodic ⇒ eliminate α between the two eventual-periodicity relations to get a monic integer polynomial vanishing at β=3/2 — contradiction (Parry's monicity trick extended to the intermediate two-kneading case). This upgrades 'no SFT' to 'no sofic', i.e., no finite-state symbolic model anywhere on the Collatz fiber.

finds.ref: Yun Sun, Bing Li, Yiming Ding, "Subshifts of finite type and matching for intermediate β-transformations", arXiv:2211.15239 (2022); companion: Sun–Li–Ding, "Fiber denseness of intermediate β-shifts of finite type", Nonlinearity 36 (2023) 5973
finds.the_theorem: Same family on Δ={β∈(1,2), 0<α<2−β}. Theorem 1.1: F⊊M (Ω_{β,α} SFT ⇒ T_{β,α} has matching, where matching = ∃n: T^n(0)=T^n(1), same n) and closure(F(β))=closure(M(β)) on each vertical fiber; each matching parameter lies in a matching interval I(β,α), there are at most countably many matching intervals per fiber, matching intervals are pairwise disjoint, and SFT parameters are dense in each matching interval. So SFT dense in a fiber iff matching dense in that fiber. Multinacci β: the whole fiber is a single matching interval (all parameters share the same matching); for Pisot non-multinacci β the fiber never has uniform matching. Fiber-denseness paper: SFT parameters are dense along kneading-fibers Δ(k+) (curves in (β,α) that vary β).
finds.the_match: Combined with BCK's algebraic-integer obstruction: M(3/2)=∅, hence F(3/2)=∅ — on the Collatz fiber there is no matching, no matching interval, no SFT, and their fiber-approximation theorem is vacuous exactly there. The contrast is sharp: multinacci fibers are 100% solvable, Pisot fibers are a countable-union-of-windows patchwork, the rational fiber 3/2 is 0% solvable. The 'fiber-denseness' rescue works only along kneading-fibers, which are transverse to β=const — again matching accumulates at the caricature only by moving the slope. LOOSE PART: all statements are about the symbolic/measurable structure of the interval maps; nothing about integer points; and their matching intervals per fiber being countable means even on good fibers 'solvable windows dense' can fail — density is proven only for multinacci-type cases.
finds.transfer: Use their kneading-invariant calculus to make the caricature statement effective: exhibit, for each α in a computable dense set, the initial segment where the kneading pair of T_{3/2,α} provably fails every finite-type condition (quantitative non-soficity along the fiber), and compare the growth rate of the minimal 'SFT-violation time' with our DFA-size lower-bound experiments (no regular invariant set with ≤7 states) — same quantity, two languages.

finds.ref: Carlo Carminati, Giulio Tiozzo, "Tuning and plateaux for the entropy of α-continued fractions", Nonlinearity 26 (2013) 1049–1070, arXiv:1111.2554; with C.–T., "A canonical thickening of Q and the entropy of α-continued fraction transformations", ETDS 32 (2012) 1249–1269; Nakada–Natsui, Nonlinearity 21 (2008) 1207; Kraaikamp–Schmidt–Steiner, Nonlinearity 25 (2012); Calta–Kraaikamp–Schmidt, arXiv:1701.04498
finds.the_theorem: For Nakada's α-continued fractions T_α (α∈[0,1]): matching (orbits of α and α−1 merge, with matching indices) holds on an open dense set of full Lebesgue measure; the bifurcation set E is closed, totally disconnected, measure zero with dim_H(E)=1. Matching intervals are 'maximal quadratic intervals' indexed by rational pseudocenters r; Theorem 10 (from CT ETDS): matching times on I_r are read off the continued fraction of r. Tuning paper: Theorem 1: entropy h is constant exactly on 'neutral tuning windows' W_r (complete plateau classification); Theorem 2: tuning operators τ_r map the whole parameter space into windows reversing/preserving monotonicity (Douady–Hubbard-style renormalization, self-similar parameter space); Theorem 3: complete local monotonicity classification. Calta–Kraaikamp–Schmidt: synchronization (=matching) is full measure for all α-deformations of an infinite class of CF algorithms.
finds.the_match: This is the strongest 'matching-window density' theory in existence and the template for what a solvable-window structure around a Collatz point WOULD look like: dense self-similar windows, explicit natural extensions/densities on each window, fractal exceptional set of dimension 1, and a tuning/renormalization semigroup organizing the windows. Crucial structural reason it works: the branches of T_α are Möbius with NON-CONSTANT derivative, so no algebraic-integer obstruction exists — matching indices can be nonzero and windows still open. Our caricature family has constant slope 3/2, which is exactly the degenerate case where the obstruction is fatal. LOOSE PART: no Collatz-like distinguished point exists in the α-CF family; the transfer is of formalism (tuning operators, pseudocenters, quadratic intervals), not of any specific parameter.
finds.transfer: Build the caricature's analogue of tuning: define tuning operators on kneading pairs of the (β,α) plane and prove the plane's SFT/matching windows (LSSS-dense) are organized by a Douady–Hubbard-type product with the β=3/2 fiber in the 'infinitely renormalizable' locus; then compute whether the fiber is a Lebesgue-density point of solvable windows in the plane (window density exponent at the fiber) — a measurable 'distance from solvability' for the Collatz caricature.

finds.ref: Carlo Carminati, Niels Langeveld, Wolfgang Steiner, "Tanaka–Ito α-continued fractions and matching", Nonlinearity 34 (2021) 3565–3582
finds.the_theorem: For Tanaka–Ito α-continued fractions: Theorem 1.4: matching holds Lebesgue-a.e. on [0,1] and the only matching indices are −2,0,2. Theorem 1.5: the bifurcation set E has measure zero and dim_H(E)=1, with dim_H(E∩(g,g+δ))=1 but dim_H(E∩(g+δ,1))<1. Theorem 1.6: E contains INFINITELY MANY RATIONAL parameters, E∩Q has no isolated points, and dim_H(E∩(r−δ,r+δ))>1/2 for every rational bifurcation parameter r — unlike Nakada's family, where bifurcation parameters are Liouville-like/bounded-type.
finds.the_match: First clean example where RATIONAL parameters are genuine bifurcation (non-matching) points sitting inside a fractal exceptional set with quantified local dimension. Structurally this is the closest published picture to 'a rational distinguished parameter at which solvability fails, with solvable windows accumulating from both sides at controlled Hausdorff density' — which is exactly the geometry Simon asked about, except realized in a Möbius-branch family rather than at the slope of an affine family. LOOSE PART: the rational bifurcation parameters here are shift/domain parameters, not slopes; nothing 3/2-specific; no functional of Collatz type is computed at them.
finds.transfer: Import their local-dimension technique to the (β,α) plane: estimate the Hausdorff density of SFT/matching windows in neighborhoods of points of the β=3/2 fiber (transverse accumulation exponent), and test the conjecture that the fiber consists of 'thick' bifurcation points (local window density exponent bounded away from 1) — making precise how anomalously unsolvable the Collatz fiber is compared with typical exceptional points.

finds.ref: Karma Dajani, Charlene Kalle, "Invariant measures, matching and the frequency of 0 for signed binary expansions", Publ. RIMS Kyoto (2020), arXiv:1703.06335
finds.the_theorem: Symmetric doubling maps S_η(x)=2x−dη on [−1,1], d∈{−1,0,1}, η∈[1,2]: each S_η generates signed binary expansions (digits −1,0,1). The acip density is piecewise smooth for all η outside a parameter set of ZERO Lebesgue measure and FULL Hausdorff dimension; the maximal parameter intervals of piecewise-smoothness (matching intervals) are fully described; the frequency of digit 0 in typical expansions is given explicitly on each interval, depends continuously on η, equals 2/3 exactly on the plateau η∈[6/5, 3/2], and is <2/3 elsewhere. Matching (orbits of discontinuity limits merging) is the engine; motivation includes minimal-weight NAF expansions from elliptic-curve cryptography.
finds.the_match: Integer slope 2 with a signed digit set is the caricature-adjacent family that DOES enjoy full matching theory — and it is exactly the symbolic environment of the ±1 Collatz relatives ((3n±1)/2 = signed-carry dynamics). A natural orbit-statistics functional (digit-0 frequency ≈ 'how often the counter idles') is LOCKED at the rational value 2/3 on a whole window whose right endpoint is η=3/2 — the plateau/rigidity phenomenon our cryptid functionals would need (Antihydra's counter drift +2/−1 as a functional of the parity itinerary). LOOSE PART: the appearance of 3/2 as plateau endpoint is a parameter value of a slope-2 family, not the slope — likely numerology, flagged as such; and typical-point statements again exclude integer orbits.
finds.transfer: Deform the Antihydra rule inside a 1-parameter signed-digit family: replace floor(3n/2) parity dynamics by S_η-driven counter dynamics and compute the halting-counter drift as a function of η; conjecture (and verify numerically) plateaus on matching intervals with the true Antihydra sitting at a plateau endpoint/bifurcation parameter — giving the first 'parameter-family portrait' of a BB cryptid with solvable windows around it.

finds.ref: Charlene Kalle, Niels Langeveld, Marta Maggioni, Sara Munday, "Matching for a family of infinite measure continued fraction transformations", Discrete Contin. Dyn. Syst. 40 (2020) 6309–6330, arXiv:1912.10680
finds.the_theorem: For a 1-parameter family of continued-fraction maps with an indifferent fixed point (infinite acim, counterpart of Nakada's family): matching holds for Lebesgue-a.e. parameter; the exceptional set has Hausdorff dimension 1; on the matching region a planar natural extension exists, yielding an explicit formula for the density of the unique σ-finite acim and exact computation of Krengel entropy, return sequences, and wandering rates.
finds.the_match: Shows the matching machinery survives NULL-RECURRENT/indifferent dynamics and still delivers explicit densities plus refined return/wandering asymptotics. Cryptid relevance: Antihydra/Hydra ask whether a counter driven by the parity itinerary ever hits an exact boundary against positive drift — a return-time/wandering-rate functional of exactly the kind this paper computes on matching windows. LOOSE PART: their indifferent fixed point produces infinite measure, whereas cryptid dynamics have exponential drift; the analogy is 'functionals of orbits computed exactly on matching windows', not a shared phase portrait.
finds.transfer: For deformations of the caricature that create a neutral fixed point (δ→boundary), compute wandering-rate/return-sequence functionals exactly on matching windows and track their degeneration toward the Collatz-like parameter — a controlled approach to 'heuristic halting probabilities' like (1/φ)^n via explicit natural extensions instead of coin-flip models.

finds.ref: Rainer Klages, J.R. Dorfman, "Simple maps with fractal diffusion coefficients", Phys. Rev. Lett. 74 (1995) 387; Gerhard Keller, Phil Howard, Rainer Klages, "Continuity properties of transport coefficients in simple maps", Nonlinearity 21 (2008) 1719, arXiv:0801.2413
finds.the_theorem: For lifted piecewise-linear expanding maps (slope λ as parameter): the diffusion coefficient D(λ) is computable in closed form on a DENSE set of Markov-partition parameters (topological transition matrices) and its graph is a fractal-like object. Keller–Howard–Klages (rigorous, for piecewise C² expanding interval-map families with exponentially mixing transfer operators): the drift J(λ) and diffusion coefficient D(λ) are continuous with modulus of continuity O(|δλ|·(log|δλ|)²) — Lipschitz up to quadratic-log corrections — with strong local variation and typically no differentiability; sharper estimates at specific Markov parameters.
finds.the_match: Statistical-physics venue instance of the exact signature: a physically meaningful functional of a piecewise-affine family, exactly solvable on a dense (Markov='matching-like') parameter set, fractal on the exceptional structure, with a rigorous regularity theorem for the functional across the family. Our cryptid heuristics (halting probability (1/φ)^n for Antihydra, drift of the counter) are precisely drift/diffusion functionals of parity-itinerary dynamics. LOOSE PART: their theory concerns Lebesgue-acim statistics of real orbits; cryptid questions live on single integer orbits (measure zero) and on hitting EXACT boundaries, which no acim functional sees; transfer is at the annealed/typical-model level only.
finds.transfer: Define the lifted caricature family and compute J(δ), D(δ) for the counter functional (+2 on even, −1 on odd) as functions of δ; KHK guarantees log²-Lipschitz continuity, Markov parameters give exact values on a dense set; test whether the Antihydra heuristic (1/φ)^n survival exponent equals the value of the annealed large-deviation functional at the true parameter and how it varies across the family (is the cryptid at a local extremum/plateau?).

best_single_bet: The Bruin–Carminati–Kalle / Li–Sahlsten–Samuel–Steiner algebraic-integer obstruction, played as a non-soficity theorem for the whole Collatz fiber. One week plan, first move: write and machine-check the α-elimination lemma — if both kneading invariants of T_{3/2,α}(x)=(3/2)x+α mod 1 are eventually periodic, eliminate α between the two eventual-periodicity relations (resultant) and show the resulting integer polynomial in β is monic, contradicting β=3/2 not being an algebraic integer; verify monicity symbolically for all small period/preperiod pairs in a CAS before proving it in general. Payoff ladder: (i) 'no (3/2,α) intermediate β-shift is sofic' — every finite-state symbolic model is impossible on the entire caricature fiber, for every offset δ, upgrading BCK's no-matching remark; (ii) then attempt the bridge to the integer problem: show a regular (DFA) invariant set of the base-2 Collatz transduction avoiding 1 would induce, via the transpose/base-conversion correspondence (Sterin–Woods machinery already in our library), a sofic certificate for slope-3/2 kneading dynamics — which (i) forbids — turning our empirical '≤7 DFA states, none exist' into 'no regular divergence certificate at any size'. Step (ii) is where the caricature gap lives (LSB-first 2-adic coding vs MSB-first interval coding are transposed, not conjugate systems) and may fail; step (i) alone is already a publishable, fully rigorous 'what exactly fails at the Collatz parameter' theorem.
dead_ends: (1) A family where solvable/matching parameters accumulate at the Collatz caricature WITHIN its own fiber: provably empty hunt — BCK (Indag. 2017, Sec. 3) and BCMP (Nonlinearity 2019, Thm 3) show matching in constant-slope affine families forces the slope to be an algebraic integer ('matching cannot hold for any non-integer rational value s'), so the entire β=3/2 fiber has no matching for any offset; do not re-search 'matching for (3/2)x+α'. (2) Accumulation of proven-solvable SLOPES at 3/2 from both sides: Pisot slopes cannot accumulate there (Pisot set is closed, Salem 1944, and 3/2 is not Pisot); Salem-slope matching prevalence is unproven even for single examples (BCK say 'unclear'); only the 2-parameter SFT-denseness (LSSS PAMS 2019) gives two-sided accumulation, necessarily moving β. (3) The framing 'the Collatz fiber is THE unique exceptional fiber' is wrong: transcendental-β fibers are also SFT-free (LSSS); what is special at 3/2 is the arithmetic mechanism (3^m ≡ 1 mod 2 obstruction; kneading orbits live in Z[1/2] with genuine Collatz-style denominator dynamics), not bare unsolvability. (4) A point CONJUGATE to the 2-adic Collatz map inside these interval families: impossible, not just unfound — entropy of T_{β,α} is log β ≤ log 2 with β=3/2 giving log(3/2), while the 2-adic Collatz map is conjugate to the full 2-shift (entropy log 2); only itinerary-caricatures, never conjugates, can sit in the family. (5) β-encoder/A-D-converter engineering literature (Daubechies–Güntürk et al.): their robustness windows come from greedy/lazy expansion freedom (univoque theory), not matching — dropped as a false positive of the signature. (6) Hidden Collatz awareness in the matching community: greped all 11 downloaded papers for Collatz/3x+1 — zero mentions anywhere; the community is genuinely blind (good for novelty; also means no ready-made bridge lemmas exist).
